dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a priority 1 chest pain.
Hi, I'm Alex. What made you call 911 tonight?
Chest pain, started two days ago and it's gotten progressively worse.
Can you tell me your name and where you are right now?
Ma'am, can I check your pulse?
I'm going to take your blood pressure now.
I'll check your oxygen levels on the fingertip device.
I'd like to monitor your breathing rate for a moment.
I'll check your head, neck, chest, back for deformities or skin injuries. No deformities, lacerations, or discolorations found.
Can you tell me about any existing health conditions? You've mentioned diabetes and endometriosis.
What medications are you taking? Metformin and vitamin D.
Are you allergic to any medications? You said you're allergic to penicillin.
I'll check your pupils now. Both appear equal and reactive.
We've placed you on a cardiac monitor using four leads.
We have your 12-lead EKG and it's showing normal sinus rhythm (NSR).
Your blood sugar is at 152.
I've just given you 324mg of aspirin (ASA).
Your motor and sensory responses are intact and equal in both arms and legs-PMS present.
Your peripheral pulses are strong and equal on both sides.
I've started your IV in the right arm to be ready if more medications are needed.
Please help me place you on the stretcher and secure you with the belts for transport.
The patient is ambulatory and helped herself into the ambulance.
Patient is alert with warm, dry skin and normal breathing. No tracheal deviation or facial droop.
46-year-old female with 10/10 chest pain radiating to the left jaw. She is oriented times 4 (AOx4) with normal mental status.
Vital signs: 114, 18, 153/82, 100%, 152. An hour later: 86, 18, 153/79, 99%. She denies nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
12-lead EKG was normal, IV in place in the right arm. No slurred speech or facial droop noted.
Final pulse was 76, 18 breaths per minute, oxygen saturation 98%.
I'll call ahead to inform the ED nurse so we can make a smooth handoff.
All set. Patient arrived safely and we'll be back in service shortly.
